title: Artocarpus muricatus
taxonomy: Artocarpus muricatus is a species of breadfruit tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia. This species belongs to the family Moraceae, which includes other plants like figs and mulberries. Within the family Moraceae, Artocarpus muricatus is classified under the genus Artocarpus, a group of trees known for their edible fruits. The genus Artocarpus is further divided into several species, each with unique characteristics. By understanding its classification, we can better appreciate the diversity of plant life in tropical ecosystems.
uses: Artocarpus muricatus is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The roots and leaves of this plant are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some cultures, Artocarpus muricatus is also used as an antidote for snake bites and other poisonous stings. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential health benefits and applications.

intro: Artocarpus muricatus, also known as the Java breadfruit or Singapore cherry, is a species of tree in the mulberry family (Moraceae) native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Artocarpus muricatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
